option.name: name
option.value: Fix issue from Claude on GitHub

option.name: text
option.value:
I want you to fix an issue in a repository. First, ask me for the link to the GitHub issue I want to fix.
Once I provide it:
1. **Read the GitHub issue** and understand the problem described.
2. **Clone the repository** (use the default branch) and **examine the actual code**. Verify whether the issue is still valid and actually needs fixing. If you determine it's a false positive or already fixed, tell me and stop.
3. **Evaluate the suggested fix** if one is provided in the issue. Assess whether it will work correctly and whether it could introduce any regressions or new issues.
4. **Consider your own solution.** If you come up with an alternative approach, compare both solutions and explain which is better and why. Pick the best one.
5. **Implement the fix.** Make the code changes, and if the issue mentions new tests, implement those too.
**Path hint:** If you can't find a file at the path mentioned in the issue, try replacing `java` with `kotlin` in the path (e.g., `backend/src/main/java/eu/.../Messaging.kt` → `backend/src/main/kotlin/eu/.../Messaging.kt`), or vice versa.
**Important constraints — do NOT:** - Change `minSdk`, `targetSdk`, `compileSdk`, or any other SDK/platform version settings - Upgrade or downgrade major dependency versions (minor/patch security fixes are OK) - Change the servlet version, App Engine runtime, or any deployment target - Change supported device range or platform compatibility in any way - Make large-scale refactors — keep changes minimal and surgical, focused only on the issue
6. **Verify your changes:** - Build the project (if a build system is available) - Run existing tests to confirm no regressions - Run any new tests you added
7. **Do NOT commit.** Instead, prepare a commit message I can use. The commit message must: - Include a `Fixes #<issue_number>` keyword so that the GitHub issue is automatically closed when the commit is merged - End with a line stating it was done by AI and which model was used (e.g., `AI-assisted fix by <model_name>`)
Show me the suggested commit message at the end.
Ask me if anything is unclear before you start.
